title: Globba corneri
taxonomy: Globba corneri is classified within the family Zingiberaceae and is a species of plant in the genus Globba. Globba corneri is native to the Western Ghats region of India and is commonly found in the tropical forests of the area. The plant is a member of the order Zingiberales and is closely related to other plants in the Zingiberaceae family. Globba corneri is often used in traditional medicine and is prized for its unique flowers and fragrance. The plant's classification is further divided into the family, genus, and species levels, with Globba corneri being the specific species within the genus Globba.

intro: Globba corneri, also known as the Ginger Lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Zingiberaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine to provide various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in reducing pain and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Globba corneri include gingerol derivatives, sesquiterpenes, and other compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties.
